drugs.notes: Twice daily dosing (BD); doses given 12 hours apart. Infused in 1000ml sodium chloride 0.9% over 240 minutes. Dose banded according to national dose bands (100mg/ml). Standard dose is 3000mg/m²; reduced to 1500mg/m² in elderly or relapsed post-transplant patients.

dose_modifications.condition: Hepatic impairment: bilirubin greater than 34 µmol/L
dose_modifications.notes: Reduce to 50% of original dose. Escalate doses in subsequent cycles in the absence of toxicity.

dose_modifications.condition: Renal impairment: creatinine clearance less than 60 ml/min
dose_modifications.notes: Reduce to 60% of original dose.

dose_modifications.condition: Renal impairment: creatinine clearance less than 45 ml/min
dose_modifications.notes: Reduce to 50% of original dose.

dose_modifications.condition: Renal impairment: creatinine clearance less than 30 ml/min
dose_modifications.notes: Discuss with consultant before prescribing.

dose_modifications.condition: Haematological: neutrophils less than 1x10⁹/L or platelets less than 100x10⁹/L
dose_modifications.notes: Delay treatment; check with relevant consultant. Consider blood transfusion if Hb less than 8g/dL.

pre_medications: Ondansetron 8mg twice daily oral or IV for 7 days (starting 15-30 minutes prior to chemotherapy)
Metoclopramide 10mg three times daily when required oral or IV
Aciclovir 400mg twice daily oral until neutrophils greater than 1x10⁹/L
Prednisolone 0.5% eye drops 1 drop each eye four times daily (continue for 5 days after cytarabine administration is complete)
Allopurinol 300mg once daily oral for 7 days (consider for induction; generally not required in consolidation setting)
Antifungal prophylaxis: discuss need and choice with consultant
Mouthwashes according to local or national policy for mucositis
Gastric protection with PPI or H2 antagonist for high-risk GI patients
special_warnings: Inpatient regimen — all supportive medications must be prescribed on the inpatient chart or general electronic prescribing system.
Cytarabine is administered TWICE daily; doses must be given 12 hours apart.
Prednisolone 0.5% eye drops are mandatory prophylaxis against cytarabine-induced conjunctivitis; continue for 5 days after last cytarabine dose.
Monitor for cerebellar toxicity (dizziness, confusion, ataxia) during and after high-dose cytarabine therapy.
Cytarabine is a neutral agent but irritant in large volumes — extravasation precautions apply.
Cycle 2, if required, should proceed only after neutrophil and platelet recovery and assessment of response to cycle 1.
Third dose reduction requires treatment cessation. Dose reductions should not be re-escalated without consultant approval.
Consider blood transfusion if patient is symptomatic of anaemia or has haemoglobin less than 8g/dL.

drugs.notes: Twice daily dosing (BD); doses given 12 hours apart. Infused in 1000ml sodium chloride 0.9% over 240 minutes. Dose banded according to national dose bands (100mg/ml). Standard dose for good-risk AML consolidation; may be decreased to 1500mg/m² in elderly or relapsed post-transplant patients.

dose_modifications.condition: Hepatic impairment: bilirubin greater than 34 µmol/L
dose_modifications.notes: Reduce to 50% of original dose. Escalate doses in subsequent cycles in the absence of toxicity.

dose_modifications.condition: Renal impairment: creatinine clearance less than 60 ml/min
dose_modifications.notes: Reduce to 60% of original dose.

dose_modifications.condition: Renal impairment: creatinine clearance less than 45 ml/min
dose_modifications.notes: Reduce to 50% of original dose.

dose_modifications.condition: Renal impairment: creatinine clearance less than 30 ml/min
dose_modifications.notes: Discuss with consultant before prescribing.

dose_modifications.condition: Haematological: neutrophils less than 1x10⁹/L or platelets less than 100x10⁹/L
dose_modifications.notes: Delay treatment; check with relevant consultant. Consider blood transfusion if Hb less than 8g/dL.

pre_medications: Ondansetron 8mg twice daily oral or IV for 7 days (starting 15-30 minutes prior to chemotherapy)
Metoclopramide 10mg three times daily when required oral or IV
Aciclovir 400mg twice daily oral until neutrophils greater than 1x10⁹/L
Prednisolone 0.5% eye drops 1 drop each eye four times daily (continue for 5 days after cytarabine administration is complete)
Allopurinol 300mg once daily oral for 7 days (consider for induction; generally not required in consolidation setting)
Antifungal prophylaxis: discuss need and choice with consultant
Mouthwashes according to local or national policy for mucositis
Gastric protection with PPI or H2 antagonist for high-risk GI patients
special_warnings: Inpatient regimen — all supportive medications must be prescribed on the inpatient chart or general electronic prescribing system.
Cytarabine is administered TWICE daily; doses must be given 12 hours apart.
Prednisolone 0.5% eye drops are mandatory prophylaxis against cytarabine-induced conjunctivitis; continue for 5 days after last cytarabine dose.
Monitor for cerebellar toxicity (dizziness, confusion, headache, ataxia) during and after high-dose cytarabine therapy.
Cytarabine is a neutral agent but irritant in large volumes — extravasation precautions apply.
Cycle 2, if required, should proceed only after neutrophil and platelet recovery and assessment of response to cycle 1.
Third dose reduction requires treatment cessation. Dose reductions should not be re-escalated without consultant approval.
Consider blood transfusion if patient is symptomatic of anaemia or has haemoglobin less than 8g/dL.

drugs.notes: Once daily subcutaneous injection into the thigh or abdomen; rotate injection sites. Dose banded according to national dose bands (20mg/ml). Cycle 1: days 1-10. Cycle 2 onwards: days 1-10. Cycles should ideally begin on Monday, Tuesday or Wednesday so only one weekend falls within the 10-day treatment period. May be administered in the outpatient setting.

Cycle 1 dose ramp-up: Day 1 = 100mg, Day 2 = 200mg, Day 3 = 300mg, Days 4-28 = 100mg (adjusted due to interaction with azole antifungal prophylaxis). If azole antifungal prophylaxis is contraindicated, venetoclax 600mg once daily is funded. Cycle 2 onwards: 100mg days 1-28 (or 14 days of each 28-day cycle as per NICE submission if applicable; 600mg if no azole prophylaxis). Tablets are film-coated and must be swallowed whole with a meal and water; not chewed, crushed or broken. Take at approximately the same time each day. During dose-titration phase, take in the morning. Grapefruit, Seville oranges and starfruit should be avoided.
Cycle 1 only — dose ramp-up day 2. See main venetoclax entry for full titration schedule.
Cycle 1 only — dose ramp-up day 3. See main venetoclax entry for full titration schedule.

dose_modifications.condition: Hepatic impairment (Cytarabine): bilirubin greater than 34 µmol/L
dose_modifications.notes: Reduce cytarabine to 50% of original dose. Escalate in subsequent cycles in absence of toxicity.

dose_modifications.condition: Renal impairment (Cytarabine): any degree
dose_modifications.notes: No dose adjustment necessary for cytarabine in renal impairment at this low subcutaneous dose.

dose_modifications.condition: Renal impairment (Venetoclax): CrCl 15-90 ml/min (mild to severe)
dose_modifications.notes: No dose adjustment required for mild, moderate or severe renal impairment (CrCl ≥15 ml/min). Administer with caution if CrCl 15-30 ml/min (benefit must outweigh risk). Patients with CrCl <80 ml/min may need more intensive TLS prophylaxis and monitoring.

dose_modifications.condition: Hepatic impairment (Venetoclax): mild (bilirubin normal, AST/ALT >ULN, or bilirubin 1-1.5x ULN, Child-Pugh A) or moderate (bilirubin 1.5-3x ULN, Child-Pugh B)
dose_modifications.notes: No dose adjustment required but monitor more closely. Trend for increased adverse events in moderate hepatic impairment.

dose_modifications.condition: Hepatic impairment (Venetoclax): severe (Child-Pugh C or bilirubin >3x ULN)
dose_modifications.notes: Reduce venetoclax by at least 50% throughout treatment. Monitor closely for toxicity.

dose_modifications.condition: Grade 3-4 hepatotoxicity (ALT, AST or bilirubin grade 3-4)
dose_modifications.notes: Withhold venetoclax (including azole antifungals). If no improvement to grade 2 or below, continue to withhold. May restart at original dose once resolved to grade 2 or below.

dose_modifications.condition: Haematologic toxicity (Grade 4 neutropenia or grade 4 thrombocytopenia) — prior to achieving remission
dose_modifications.notes: In most instances, do not interrupt venetoclax in combination with cytarabine.

dose_modifications.condition: Haematologic toxicity (Grade 4 neutropenia or grade 4 thrombocytopenia) — first occurrence after remission, lasting at least 7 days
dose_modifications.notes: Delay subsequent cycle of venetoclax+cytarabine; monitor blood counts. Administer G-CSF if indicated. Resume at same dose on resolution to grade 1 or 2.

dose_modifications.condition: Haematologic toxicity (Grade 4 neutropenia or grade 4 thrombocytopenia) — subsequent occurrences after remission, lasting 7+ days
dose_modifications.notes: Delay subsequent cycle; administer G-CSF if indicated. On resolution to grade 1-2, resume at same dose and reduce venetoclax duration by 7 days per subsequent cycle (e.g. 21 days instead of 28 days).

dose_modifications.condition: TLS — biochemical changes: dose interruption with restart after >48 hours resolution
dose_modifications.notes: Restart at reduced dose per Table 2: 400mg→300mg; 300mg→200mg; 200mg→100mg; 100mg→50mg; 50mg→20mg; 20mg→10mg. TLS prophylaxis instructions must be followed on resumption.

dose_modifications.condition: Other Grade 3-4 toxicity related to venetoclax
dose_modifications.notes: Withhold until toxicity resolves to grade 2 or below, then restart at original dose.

pre_medications: Metoclopramide 10mg three times daily when required oral (take-home antiemetic)
Allopurinol 300mg once daily oral for 28 days — start 72 hours prior to first venetoclax dose (cycle 1)

Antifungal prophylaxis (choice dependent on local formulary): Posaconazole tablets 300mg twice daily on Day 4 then 300mg once daily thereafter; OR Voriconazole tablets 400mg twice daily on Day 4 then 200mg twice daily thereafter
Adequate hydration: patients instructed to drink 1.5-2L water daily starting 2 days before venetoclax and throughout dose-titration phase; IV fluids if oral hydration inadequate
Anti-hyperuricaemic agents prior to initiation of first venetoclax dose and during dose-titration phase
Viral screening (Hepatitis B surface antigen, core antibody, HIV) before starting treatment
special_warnings: TUMOUR LYSIS SYNDROME (TLS): Critical risk with venetoclax. All patients must have WBC <25x10⁹/L prior to initiation; cytoreduction may be required. Correct pre-existing electrolyte abnormalities before starting. Monitor potassium, uric acid, phosphate, adjusted calcium, LDH and creatinine at 6-8 hours and 24 hours after first dose and after each dose increase. Next venetoclax dose must not be given until 24-hour blood chemistry has been evaluated.
Hospitalisation is recommended for the first 5 days of cycle 1, during venetoclax dose titration, and for 24 hours after dose titration.
Venetoclax dose ramp-up in cycle 1: Day 1 = 100mg, Day 2 = 200mg, Day 3 = 300mg, Days 4-28 = 100mg (with azole prophylaxis) or 600mg (without azole prophylaxis).

Venetoclax interacts significantly with azole antifungals (posaconazole, voriconazole) — dose is reduced to 100mg when used concomitantly. If azole prophylaxis is contraindicated, venetoclax 600mg is funded.
Grapefruit products, Seville oranges and starfruit (carambola) must be avoided during venetoclax treatment.
Venetoclax must only be prescribed under the supervision of a consultant haematologist.
The National Patient Safety Alert on oral chemotherapy (NPSA/2008/RRR001) must be followed for venetoclax.
Many drug interactions associated with venetoclax — always check interactions, including timing with TKIs.
Formal medical review as to whether venetoclax should continue must occur at least by end of second cycle.
If venetoclax is stopped for any reason (disease progression, unacceptable toxicity, patient consent withdrawal, sustained complete remission), no further venetoclax can be prescribed.
Patients with risk factors for TLS (circulating blasts, high bone marrow involvement, elevated LDH, reduced renal function) require additional monitoring and may need a reduced starting dose.

TLS management table: Hyperkalaemia — hold venetoclax and manage per local hyperkalaemia policy; Hyperuricaemia (uric acid ≥476 µmol/L) — hold, consider rasburicase; Hypocalcaemia (adjusted calcium <1.75 mmol/L) — consider holding, give calcium gluconate; Hyperphosphataemia — withhold and manage per severity; Creatinine rise ≥25% from baseline — hold and administer IV fluids.

drugs.notes: Twice daily (BD) subcutaneous injection; doses given 12 hours apart. Inject into the thigh or abdomen; rotate injection sites. 28-42 day cycle for at least 4 cycles (28-day cycle set in ARIA). Subcutaneous cytarabine may be administered in the outpatient setting providing 12-hourly administration can be arranged. Cycles should ideally begin on Monday, Tuesday or Wednesday so only one weekend is included in each 10-day cycle.

dose_modifications.condition: Hepatic impairment: bilirubin greater than 34 µmol/L
dose_modifications.notes: Reduce to 50% of original dose. Escalate doses in subsequent cycles in the absence of toxicity.

dose_modifications.condition: Renal impairment: any degree
dose_modifications.notes: No dose modification required for renal impairment at this low subcutaneous dose.

dose_modifications.condition: Non-haematological NCI-CTC grade 3 or above toxicity
dose_modifications.notes: Delay treatment until adverse effect resolves to NCI-CTC grade 1 or below. Dose of causative agent may then be reduced or discontinued at discretion of consultant.

dose_modifications.condition: Haematological toxicity
dose_modifications.notes: Consider blood transfusion or erythropoietin if symptomatic anaemia or Hb <8g/dL (80g/L).

pre_medications: Allopurinol 300mg once daily oral — cycle 1; may be continued on subsequent cycles depending on response and counts
Metoclopramide 10mg three times daily when required oral (antiemetic)
special_warnings: Cytarabine is administered TWICE daily; doses must be given 12 hours apart.
Minimum of 4 cycles planned; cycle length 28-42 days.

Outpatient administration is possible provided 12-hourly scheduling can be arranged; clear arrangements for medical authorisation, support, problem-solving and emergency action must be in place between community services and the referring hospital.
For AML patients aged 60 years and over and/or those not fit enough for intensive therapy.
For MDS patients with intermediate, high risk or very high risk disease (IPSS-R).
Also for leukaemia relapsed post transplant.
Third dose reduction requires treatment cessation. Dose reductions should not be re-escalated without consultant approval.

protocol_name: AML Inpatient DA (2+5) — Daunorubicin + Cytarabine Consolidation

drugs.notes: Intravenous infusion in 100ml sodium chloride 0.9% over 60 minutes, or slow IV bolus over 20 minutes (infusion set as default in ARIA). Final concentration must NOT exceed 1mg/mL. Daunorubicin is a vesicant. Dose banded according to national dose bands (5mg/ml). Recommended maximum cumulative lifetime dose: 500-600mg/m² (or 400mg/m² in patients with cardiac dysfunction or exposed to mediastinal irradiation).
Twice daily (BD) IV bolus; doses given 12 hours apart. Dose banded according to national dose bands (20mg/ml). Cytarabine is a neutral agent.

dose_modifications.condition: Hepatic impairment (Daunorubicin): bilirubin 25-50 µmol/L
dose_modifications.notes: Reduce daunorubicin to 75% of original dose.

dose_modifications.condition: Hepatic impairment (Daunorubicin): bilirubin 51-85 µmol/L
dose_modifications.notes: Reduce daunorubicin to 50% of original dose.

dose_modifications.condition: Hepatic impairment (Daunorubicin): bilirubin greater than 85 µmol/L
dose_modifications.notes: Omit daunorubicin.

dose_modifications.condition: Hepatic impairment (Cytarabine): bilirubin greater than 34 µmol/L
dose_modifications.notes: Reduce cytarabine to 50% of original dose. Escalate in subsequent cycles in absence of toxicity.

dose_modifications.condition: Renal impairment (Daunorubicin): creatinine less than 105 µmol/L
dose_modifications.notes: Full dose.

dose_modifications.condition: Renal impairment (Daunorubicin): creatinine 105-265 µmol/L
dose_modifications.notes: Reduce daunorubicin to 75% of original dose.

dose_modifications.condition: Renal impairment (Daunorubicin): creatinine greater than 265 µmol/L
dose_modifications.notes: Reduce daunorubicin to 50% of original dose.

dose_modifications.condition: Renal impairment (Cytarabine): any degree
dose_modifications.notes: Dose reduction not normally necessary as doses not considered high dose.

dose_modifications.condition: Haematological toxicity
dose_modifications.notes: Delay treatment for 3 days at a time if haematological parameters not met; dose delays preferred over dose reductions. Discuss with consultant.

pre_medications: Ondansetron 8mg twice daily oral or IV on days 1, 2, 3, 4, 5 (starting 15-30 minutes prior to chemotherapy)
Metoclopramide 10mg three times daily when required oral or IV
Aciclovir 400mg twice daily oral
Antifungal prophylaxis: discuss need and choice with consultant haematologist
Mouthwashes according to local or national policy for mucositis
Gastric protection with PPI or H2 antagonist for high-risk GI patients
special_warnings: Inpatient regimen — all supportive medications must be prescribed on the inpatient chart or general electronic prescribing system.
Daunorubicin is a VESICANT — extravasation must be avoided. Final concentration must NOT exceed 1mg/mL.
Cumulative lifetime dose limit for daunorubicin: 500-600mg/m² (or 400mg/m² in patients with cardiac dysfunction or mediastinal irradiation). Monitor total anthracycline exposure.
Cytarabine is administered TWICE daily; doses must be given 12 hours apart.

Baseline MUGA scan or echocardiogram recommended for patients at risk of impaired cardiac function (cardiac history, hypertension, obesity, smoking, elderly, prior anthracycline exposure, prior thoracic radiotherapy). Repeat if cardiac toxicity suspected or cumulative dose approaches maximum.
Daunorubicin is an anthracycline with potential for chronic and acute cardiac failure and dysrhythmias.
Dose delays rather than dose reductions are recommended for haematological toxicity; discuss all reductions/delays with consultant.
Third dose reduction requires treatment cessation.

protocol_name: AML Ambulatory DA (3+8) CADD — Daunorubicin + Cytarabine (CADD Pump) Consolidation

drugs.notes: Intravenous infusion in 100ml sodium chloride 0.9% over 60 minutes, or slow IV bolus over 20 minutes. Final concentration must NOT exceed 1mg/mL. Daunorubicin is a vesicant. Dose banded according to national dose bands (5mg/ml). Recommended maximum cumulative lifetime dose: 600mg/m² (or 400mg/m² in patients with cardiac dysfunction or extensive pelvic radiation). Given on Days 1, 3 and 5.

Twice daily (BD) IV bolus via CADD pump in 125ml sodium chloride 0.9%. Doses given 12 hours apart. Dose banded according to national dose bands (100mg/ml). Each CADD pump contains 8 doses (16 doses total = two pumps). Pump quantity equals 800mg/m² diluted in sodium chloride 0.9% 125ml (15ml per dose plus 5ml overage). First dose administered on evening of Day 1; pump changed on Day 5 after morning dose. This regimen should be given after a cycle of DA (3+10). Day 1 begins when neutrophil ≥1x10⁹/L and platelets ≥80x10⁹/L. Ambulatory regimen — patient fulfils criteria for ambulatory care.

dose_modifications.condition: Hepatic impairment (Cytarabine): bilirubin greater than 34 µmol/L
dose_modifications.notes: Reduce cytarabine to 50% of original dose. Escalate in subsequent cycles in absence of toxicity.

dose_modifications.notes: Reduce daunorubicin to 75% of original dose.

dose_modifications.notes: Reduce daunorubicin to 50% of original dose.

dose_modifications.condition: Renal impairment (Daunorubicin): creatinine greater than 265 µmol/L
dose_modifications.notes: Reduce daunorubicin to 50% of original dose.

dose_modifications.condition: Haematological toxicity
dose_modifications.notes: Delay treatment; dose delays preferred over dose reductions. Discuss with consultant. Treatment should restart once haematological parameters are met.

pre_medications: Ondansetron 8mg oral or IV on Day 1 (given prior to daunorubicin); ondansetron 8mg twice daily starting evening of Day 1 for 8 days oral (take-home)
Metoclopramide 10mg three times daily when required oral (take-home)
Aciclovir 400mg twice daily for 28 days (take-home)
Mouthwashes according to local or national policy for mucositis
Gastric protection with PPI or H2 antagonist for high-risk GI patients
special_warnings: Ambulatory regimen — patient must fulfil criteria for ambulatory care.
This regimen is given AFTER a cycle of DA (3+10). Day 1 begins only when neutrophils ≥1x10⁹/L and platelets ≥80x10⁹/L.
Daunorubicin is a VESICANT — extravasation must be avoided. Final concentration must NOT exceed 1mg/mL.
Cumulative lifetime dose limit for daunorubicin: 600mg/m² (or 400mg/m² in patients with cardiac dysfunction or extensive pelvic radiation). Monitor total anthracycline exposure.

Cytarabine is administered TWICE daily via CADD pump; doses given 12 hours apart. Each pump contains 8 doses; two pumps required (16 doses total = 800mg/m² per pump). Pump changed on Day 5 after the morning dose.
Cytarabine via CADD pump: diluted in 125ml sodium chloride 0.9% (15ml per dose plus 5ml overage).
Baseline MUGA scan or echocardiogram recommended for patients at cardiac risk. Repeat if toxicity suspected or cumulative dose approaches maximum.
Daunorubicin is an anthracycline with potential for chronic and acute cardiac failure and dysrhythmias.
Monitor blood pressure, fluid balance and weight throughout and immediately after the 6-day administration period.
Third dose reduction requires treatment cessation.
